name: Software subscriptions (SaaS)
summary: Consumer software subscriptions — productivity, streaming, security, cloud storage.

evidence.claim: Average US household spends $273/month on subscriptions but estimates their subscription spend at ~$86/month — a 3x underestimate. Forgotten or unused subscriptions drive the gap.
California SB-313 requires online-signup subscriptions to permit online cancellation; NY, IL, VT have similar laws. Federal FTC Click-to-Cancel Rule was vacated July 2025.

criteria.name: annual_cost_if_yearly_plan
criteria.notes: Yearly plans typically save 15-20% but lock funds.

criteria.name: cancellation_ease
criteria.notes: In CA/NY/IL/VT, online-signup mandates online-cancel (E2). Elsewhere, check experience before buying.

criteria.name: data_ownership
criteria.notes: Can you export your data if you cancel? Lock-in risk.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Free trials require a payment method at signup 90% of the time and auto-convert silently. Calendar the end-date (E1, forced-continuity dark pattern).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is a payment method required upfront? When does auto-charge begin?

confabulationPatterns.reality: 'Anytime' frequently means 'after navigating through a roach-motel'. Check for online-cancel availability before signing up.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Can the subscription be cancelled in <5 clicks online?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Annual billing does save money, but locks in ~12 months of commitment. If the service is discontinued or features remove, you cannot pro-rate refund.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Will the user use the service for all 12 months? If any doubt, monthly is safer.

compatibilityQuestions: Will you use it enough to justify? Households overestimate subscription usage by 2-3x on average (E1).
Jurisdiction: CA/NY/IL/VT residents have click-to-cancel legal rights; elsewhere, check cancellation UX first.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Feature tier escalation (start basic, upsell to premium)
